info.title: Dryade API
info.description:
# Dryade - Multi-Agent Orchestration Platform

A production-ready multi-agent platform optimized for Jetson Thor hardware.

## Features

- **Multi-Agent Execution**: Execute complex workflows with CrewAI, LangChain, ADK, or A2A agents
- **Four Execution Modes**: CHAT (direct), CREW (multi-agent), FLOW (predefined), PLANNER (dynamic)
- **Semantic Caching**: Two-tier cache with exact hash matching and embedding-based similarity
- **Self-Healing**: Automatic retry with circuit breakers and LLM reflection for error recovery
- **Sandbox Isolation**: gVisor, Docker, or process isolation for tool execution
- **RAG Pipeline**: Knowledge base with document upload and semantic search

## Authentication

JWT-based authentication (optional). Set JWT_SECRET to enable.

## Streaming

Chat endpoints support SSE streaming. Set `Accept: text/event-stream` header.

get.description:
Return core version and compatibility information.

Used by:
- marketplace: checks api_version >= its MIN_CORE_VERSION
- Health checks and monitoring
Prometheus metrics endpoint.

Check whether initial setup has been completed.

Returns the overall configured flag (based on setup_completed, NOT key
validity) and per-step completion details for the wizard UI.

post.description:
Validate an LLM provider API key in real time.

Attempts a lightweight API call (list models) against the provider.
Returns the result with model list on success or error on failure.

Save configuration and mark setup as done.

Validates that required fields are present, persists the config,
and sets setup_completed=true so the wizard never shows again.

Register a new user.

Creates a new user account with email and password,
returning access and refresh tokens on success.

Refresh access token.

Exchanges a valid refresh token for new access and refresh tokens.

Logout current user.

Logs the logout event. Client is responsible for clearing tokens.
Returns 200 on successful logout.

Create first admin user.

Only works when no users exist in the system.
Used for initial system setup.

get.description:
Get current user's profile.

Returns the profile information for the authenticated user.

patch.description:
Update current user's profile.

Allows updating display_name, avatar_url, and preferences.

get.description:
Search users by email or display name.

Case-insensitive partial match on email or display_name.
Limited to 10 results by default (max 50).

Args:
    q: Search query string (min 2 characters)
    limit: Maximum results to return (1-50)

Returns:
    List of matching users

schema.title: Response Search Users Api Users Search Get

get.description:
List all users (admin only).

Returns all user accounts in the system.
Requires admin role.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Users Get

post.description:
Create or update an invite for an email address.

Note: This stores an invite record only (no email delivery).

get.description:
Get user's model configuration.

Returns the user's current model preferences or defaults if not configured.

patch.description:
Update user's model configuration.

Creates config if not exists, otherwise updates specified fields.
Clears LLM cache to ensure changes take effect immediately.

get.description:
List supported providers with user's key status.

Returns all supported providers with information about whether
the user has stored an API key for each.

schema.title: Response List Providers Api Models Providers Get

get.description:
List user's stored API keys (prefixes only, never full keys).

Returns metadata about stored keys including provider, prefix,
and whether it's a global or model-specific key.

schema.title: Response List Keys Api Models Keys Get

post.description:
Store encrypted API key for a provider.

Keys are encrypted at rest using Fernet (AES-128-CBC).
Replaces existing key for the same provider/model combination.

delete.description:
Delete a stored API key.

Args:
    provider: Provider ID to delete key for
    model_override: If specified, deletes model-specific key only

post.description:
Test API key connectivity with provider.

If api_key is not provided, uses the stored key for the provider.
Makes a real API call to the provider to verify connectivity.
Returns validation result and available models if successful.

get.description:
List all providers with capabilities and key status.

Returns metadata for all 9 providers including authentication requirements,
capabilities (LLM, embedding, vision, etc.), and whether the user has
stored an API key.

schema.title: Response List Providers Api Providers Get

get.description:
Get details for a specific provider.

Returns metadata including capabilities and whether user has stored a key.

post.description:
Test connection to a provider using real API call.

If api_key is not provided in request, uses stored key.
For local providers (Ollama, vLLM), tests connectivity to the endpoint.
For cloud providers, validates API key with real provider API call.

Returns success status, message, and available models if connection succeeds.

get.description:
Discover available models for a provider.

For cloud providers with static model lists (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google),
returns the predefined model list from the registry.

For local providers (Ollama, vLLM), dynamically queries the provider
endpoint to discover installed/available models.

For providers requiring API keys, uses stored key if available.

Args:
    provider_id: Provider identifier
    endpoint: Optional custom endpoint URL (for local providers)

Return health status for all monitored providers.

Reads from the ProviderHealthMonitor singleton stored in app.state.
Returns green/yellow/red per provider based on circuit breaker state.

Return the current user's provider fallback chain.

Returns an empty chain with enabled=False if not configured.

put.description:
Persist a new provider fallback chain for the current user.

Validates each entry has provider and model strings.
Overwrites any existing chain.

schema.title: Response Set Fallback Order Api User Provider Fallback Order Put

post.description:
Cancel an in-progress provider fallback chain for a chat session.

Sets the asyncio.Event that the execute_with_fallback caller registered
before initiating the call. This stops iteration at the next provider
boundary.

The chat route (chat.py / websocket.py) must register an event via:
    CANCEL_EVENTS[session_id] = asyncio.Event()
before calling execute_with_fallback(..., cancel_event=CANCEL_EVENTS[session_id]).

schema.title: Response Cancel Fallback Api Chat  Session Id  Cancel Fallback Post

get.description: List user's custom providers.

schema.title: Response List Custom Providers Api Custom Providers Get

post.description: Create a custom OpenAI-compatible provider.

patch.description: Update a custom provider. Slug stays stable.

delete.description: Delete a custom provider and clean up references.

post.description:
Cancel a running orchestration for a conversation.

Signals the orchestrator loop to stop gracefully after the current step.
Returns partial results already collected.

Returns 404 if no active orchestration exists for this conversation.

schema.title: Response Cancel Orchestration Api Chat  Conversation Id  Cancel Post

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

get.summary: Check if a conversation has an active stream
get.description:
Check whether a conversation is currently streaming.

Used by the frontend on mount to detect and rejoin active streams
after navigation away and back.

schema.title: Response Get Stream Status Api Chat  Conversation Id  Stream Status Get

post.description:
Execute a chat message through the orchestration router.

Supports four execution modes:
- **chat**: Direct LLM conversation (default)
- **crew**: Multi-agent crew execution
- **flow**: Predefined workflow execution
- **planner**: Dynamic flow generation and execution

Returns a synchronous response. For streaming, use POST /stream endpoint.

400.description: Bad request - invalid input parameters or validation failed

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

503.description: Service unavailable - dependency down or system overloaded

post.description:
Streaming chat endpoint using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

Returns a stream of events:
- **start**: Initial event with conversation_id
- **token/content**: Text chunks as they're generated
- **tool_start/tool_result**: Tool invocation events
- **thinking**: LLM reasoning steps (if enable_thinking=true)
- **complete**: Final event with metadata
- **error**: Error events if something fails
- **stream_complete**: Summary event with cache status
- **[DONE]**: Stream termination marker

Set `Accept: text/event-stream` header for streaming response.

400.description: Bad request - invalid input parameters or validation failed

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

503.description: Service unavailable - dependency down or system overloaded

get.description:
Get chat history for a conversation with pagination.

Returns messages in chronological order (oldest first).
Maximum 100 messages per request.
Users can only access their own conversations. Admins can access all.

This is a destructive operation that permanently removes all

get.description:
List available execution modes.

NOTE: Frontend 'chat' mode maps to backend ExecutionMode.ORCHESTRATE.
The orchestrator handles all messages including simple chat.
Frontend 'planner' maps to backend ExecutionMode.PLANNER.

schema.title: Response List Modes Api Chat Modes Get

post.description:
Submit a response to a pending clarification request.

Called when the frontend receives a 'clarify' event and the user provides a response.
Routes to autonomous mode handler first if there's a pending autonomous clarification,
otherwise falls back to the standard clarification handler.
Returns 404 if no pending clarification exists for the conversation.

schema.title: Response Submit Clarification Api Chat Clarify Post

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

post.description:
Submit a resolution to a pending state conflict.

Called when the frontend receives a 'state_conflict' event and the user selects a value.
Returns 404 if no pending state conflict exists.

schema.title: Response Resolve State Conflict Api Chat Resolve State Conflict Post

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

get.description:

get.description:
List all registered agents with their capabilities.

Returns agents from all loaded domains including their tools and framework info.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api Agents Get

post.description:
Upload a ZIP file containing an agent project.

The ZIP is securely extracted, validated (must contain at least one .py file),
and placed in the agents/ directory. Auto-discovery then detects the framework
and registers the agent.

The agent name is derived from the ZIP filename (sanitized).

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

post.description:
Execute a flow with streaming progress updates.

Returns SSE events for each node transition:
- **start**: Execution started with ID
- **node_start**: Node beginning execution
- **complete**: Flow finished with result
- **error**: Execution failed
- **[DONE]**: Stream complete

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

get.description:
Get execution status for a flow run.

Returns current status, progress, and active node.

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

post.description:
Resume flow execution from checkpoint.

If node_id is provided, resumes from that specific node.
Otherwise, resumes from the last available checkpoint.
Requires the flow to support CheckpointMixin.

400.description: Bad request - invalid input parameters or validation failed

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

get.description:
List all registered knowledge sources.

Returns all knowledge sources that have been uploaded and indexed,
including their metadata, status, and associated crews/agents.

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

get.description:
Get details for a specific knowledge source.

Returns metadata, status, file paths, and associations for the
requested knowledge source.

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

delete.description:
Delete a knowledge source and all associated data.

Removes the knowledge source from:
- Qdrant vector store (both new hybrid and legacy collections)
- Knowledge registry (metadata)
- File system (uploaded files)

This operation is irreversible. Returns 204 No Content on success.

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

503.description: Service unavailable - dependency down or system overloaded

post.description:
Upload and index a knowledge document.

Accepts PDF, TXT, or MD files. The document is chunked, embedded using
dense + sparse models, and stored in Qdrant for hybrid semantic search.

Optionally associate the source with specific crews or agents by passing
comma-separated IDs (e.g., crew_ids="crew1,crew2").

400.description: Bad request - invalid input parameters or validation failed

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

503.description: Service unavailable - dependency down or system overloaded

post.description:
Query knowledge sources using hybrid search with reranking.

Performs hybrid vector similarity search (dense + sparse with RRF fusion)
against indexed documents, then reranks results with a cross-encoder.
Returns document chunks above the score_threshold.

The default score_threshold of 0.3 is calibrated for RRF fusion scores
(lower than cosine similarity thresholds).

400.description: Bad request - invalid input parameters or validation failed

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

503.description: Service unavailable - dependency down or system overloaded

post.description:
Bind crew/agent associations to a knowledge source.

Updates the crew and/or agent associations for an existing knowledge source.
Pass None to leave a field unchanged, or an empty list to clear associations.

GAP-102: POST /knowledge/{id}/bind accepts crew_ids and agent_ids arrays

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

delete.description:
Unbind all crew/agent associations from a knowledge source.

Clears all crew and agent associations without deleting the source itself.
The source remains in the registry and can be re-bound later.

GAP-103: DELETE /knowledge/{id}/unbind removes crew/agent associations

404.description: Not found - requested resource does not exist

500.description: Internal server error - unexpected error occurred

get.description:
Get indexed chunks for a knowledge source.

Returns the text content of all chunks indexed for this knowledge source
by querying Qdrant directly (works even after server restart).

GAP-104: GET /knowledge/{id}/chunks returns indexed chunks for preview

post.description:
Trigger PM to check for marketplace approved plugin list updates immediately.

This is a core-as-proxy endpoint: it forwards a "check now" trigger to
PM's internal port 9472. This keeps PM's internal port hidden from the
frontend — all frontend traffic goes through core.

Architecture note: "Core does not call PM via gRPC or any RPC."
This endpoint is an EXCEPTION: it is NOT requesting approved plugin list data from PM —
it is forwarding a user-initiated "check now" trigger. The approved plugin list itself
still flows PM->core via file write. This proxy only sends a one-shot trigger.

Returns:
    200 {"status": "checking"} if PM responded
    503 {"error": "...", "status": "unavailable"} if PM unreachable

schema.title: Response Check For Updates Api Plugins Check Updates Post

get.description:
Get the status of the last approved plugin list update received from PM.

Frontend polls this to check if plugins have been updated since the
last check. Returns timestamp and version of the last update.

Returns:
    {"last_updated": iso_string | null, "version": int | null, "has_update": bool}

schema.title: Response Get Update Status Api Plugins Update Status Get

get.description: List all discovered and loaded plugins.

post.description:
Enable/disable a plugin at runtime (best-effort).

Notes:
- For pipeline extensions, this flips ExtensionRegistry enabled flags.
- For other plugins, this records an in-memory override only.
- Router mounting/unmounting is not supported at runtime; restart required for that.

get.description: Get stored plugin configuration (best-effort, in-memory).

post.description:
Install a directory plugin into the configured user plugins directory.

Supports:
- Uploading a .zip/.tar/.tar.gz/.tgz archive
- Referencing a local filesystem path (archive or directory)

Security:
- Safe extraction prevents path traversal outside the target directory.
- Only installs into the user plugins directory (never pip installs).

schema.title: Response Install Plugin Api Plugins Install Post

get.description:
Get all slot registrations.

Returns a dict mapping slot names to lists of registrations.
Only includes slots that have at least one registration.

schema.title: Response Get All Slots Api Plugins Slots Get

get.description:
Get registrations for a specific slot.

Args:
    slot_name: Name of the slot (e.g., 'workflow-sidebar')

Returns:
    List of registrations sorted by priority

Raises:
    HTTPException: If slot_name is not a valid slot

schema.title: Response Get Slot Registrations Api Plugins Slots  Slot Name  Get

delete.description: Uninstall a directory plugin by deleting it from the user plugins directory.

get.description: Get details for a specific plugin.

Get plugin system statistics.

Categories are derived dynamically from plugin manifests:
- with_ui: Plugins with UI components
- with_api: Plugins exposing API endpoints

Get all slot registrations for a specific plugin.

Args:
    name: Name of the plugin

Returns:
    Dict mapping slot names to this plugin's registrations

schema.title: Response Get Plugin Slots Api Plugins  Name  Slots Get

get.description:
Get full plugin manifest for UI loading and signature verification.

Returns the complete plugin manifest including signature so the frontend
can verify authenticity before loading the UI bundle.

Args:
    name: Plugin name

Returns:
    Full manifest dict including signature for verification

Raises:
    404: Plugin not found or has no UI

schema.title: Response Get Plugin Ui Manifest Api Plugins  Name  Ui Manifest Get

get.description:
Serve plugin UI bundle with integrity verification.

Reads the plugin's UI bundle file and verifies its SHA-256 hash against
the manifest's ui_bundle_hash. Uses fail-closed pattern: any verification
error returns 500, not the bundle.

Args:
    name: Plugin name

Returns:
    JavaScript bundle content with Content-Type: application/javascript

Raises:
    404: Plugin not found or has no UI
    500: Bundle file missing, unreadable, or hash mismatch (security alert)

Serve plugin UI stylesheet (optional).

Reads the plugin's UI styles file if present. Styles are optional so a 404 is
returned if no styles file exists rather than an error.

Args:
    name: Plugin name

Returns:
    CSS content with Content-Type: text/css

Raises:
    404: Plugin not found, has no UI, or has no styles file

post.description:
Proxy plugin signing request to PM daemon.

Asks PM to sign a local plugin directory (validates manifest, computes
hash, registers in local-plugins.json, triggers merged approved plugin list push).

Returns:
    200 {"success": true, "plugin_name": "...", "hash": "sha256:..."} on success
    503 if PM daemon is not running

schema.title: Response Sign Plugin Api Plugins Sign Post

get.description:
List locally-signed custom plugins from PM registry.

Returns:
    200 {"plugins": [...]} with list of locally registered custom plugins
    503 if PM daemon is not running

schema.title: Response List Local Plugins Api Plugins Local Get

get.description:
Return key material for the workbench to derive its bridge session key.

The workbench calls this endpoint once per session to obtain the scoped
secret it needs to replicate the server-side HMAC-SHA256 key derivation.
The returned secret is scoped per-user and does NOT expose the raw bridge key.

The workbench then derives the session key as:
    HMAC-SHA256(scoped_secret, "{sub}:{exp}")

This matches the server-side derivation in EncryptedPluginBridge._extract_session_key().

Requires: Bearer token (JWT auth).
Returns: {secret: base64str, sub: str, exp: int}

schema.title: Response Get Bridge Session Key Api Plugins Bridge Session Key Get

delete.description:
Remove a locally-signed custom plugin via PM.

Removes the plugin from PM's local registry and triggers a merged
approved plugin list re-push so core no longer loads it.

Returns:
    200 {"success": true} on success
    404 {"success": false, "error": "Plugin not found"} if not registered
    503 if PM daemon is not running

schema.title: Response Delete Local Plugin Api Plugins Local  Plugin Name  Delete

get.description:
Return the marketplace catalog filtered by optional criteria.

Each plugin includes availability (in approved plugin list) and installed status.

post.description:

Checks approved plugin list and triggers a plugin reload if the plugin directory
exists but the plugin is not currently loaded.

Validates the command exists, then executes with provided arguments.
Context includes user_id from JWT token for audit logging.

    current_user: Authenticated user from JWT

Returns:

Raises:
    404: Command not found (includes suggestions for typos)
    400: Invalid arguments
    500: Execution error

404.description: Command not found

400.description: Invalid arguments

500.description: Execution error

get.description:
List all discovered skills with eligibility status.

Skills are loaded from:
1. Bundled skills (shipped with plugins)
2. Managed skills (~/.dryade/skills/)

Later sources override earlier ones for same-named skills.

edges.description: List of edges defining execution order

plan_json.description: Wrapper for nodes/edges for UI contract

status.title: Status
status.description: Plan status: draft, approved, executing, completed, failed, cancelled

execution_count.title: Execution Count
execution_count.description: Number of times this plan has been executed

PlanResponse.title: PlanResponse
PlanResponse.description:
Plan details response.

Status lifecycle (per prior decision 03-02):
- draft: Initial state, can be modified
- approved: Reviewed and ready for execution
- executing: Currently running
- completed: Successfully finished
- failed: Terminated with error
- cancelled: Manually cancelled by user

Valid transitions:
- draft -> approved, executing, cancelled
- approved -> executing, cancelled
- executing -> completed, failed
- failed -> draft (retry), cancelled
- cancelled -> draft (retry)

example.description: Research a topic and create a summary
example.name: Research and Summarize

valid.title: Valid
valid.description: True if no blocking errors were found

PlanValidationResult.title: PlanValidationResult
PlanValidationResult.description:
Pre-execution validation result for a plan.

Returned by POST /plans/{plan_id}/validate and used internally
by execute_plan() to block execution when errors are found.

UpdatePlanRequest.title: UpdatePlanRequest
UpdatePlanRequest.description:
Request to update an existing plan.

Only plans in modifiable states (draft, approved, failed, cancelled) can be updated.
Plans in executing or completed states are immutable.

workflow_json.description: Updated workflow definition. Validated against WorkflowSchema if provided

UpdateWorkflowRequest.title: UpdateWorkflowRequest
UpdateWorkflowRequest.description:

Only draft workflows can be modified. Published and archived workflows are immutable.

WorkflowResponse.title: WorkflowResponse
WorkflowResponse.description:
Full workflow details response.

Status lifecycle (per prior decision 05-02):
- draft: Initial state, can be modified and deleted
- published: Immutable, can be executed and cloned
- archived: Immutable, hidden from default listings

Transitions: draft -> published -> archived (one-way)

status.title: Status
status.description: Workflow status: draft, published, or archived

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags for categorization

WorkflowSummary.title: WorkflowSummary
WorkflowSummary.description:
Lightweight workflow summary (excludes workflow_json for performance).

Used in list endpoints to reduce payload size. For full workflow details
including workflow_json, use GET /workflows/{id}.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Authentication endpoints. User registration, login, token refresh, and initial admin setup.

tags.name: users
tags.description: User profile management. View and update user profiles, admin user listing.

tags.name: chat
tags.description: Chat execution endpoints. Supports streaming (SSE) and non-streaming modes. Routes messages through CHAT, CREW, FLOW, or PLANNER execution modes.

tags.name: agents
tags.description: Agent registry and execution. List registered agents, query capabilities, execute tasks with specific agents.

tags.name: flows
tags.description: Predefined workflow execution. Execute multi-step flows like AnalysisFlow or CoverageFlow.

tags.name: workflows
tags.description: Workflow management CRUD. Create, update, publish, and execute ReactFlow-based workflows with versioning.

tags.name: plans
tags.description: Execution plan management. Save, modify, approve, and re-execute LLM-generated plans.

tags.name: knowledge
tags.description: Knowledge base management. Upload documents, query semantic search, manage RAG sources.

tags.name: metrics
tags.description: Performance metrics. Latency statistics, TTFT tracking, request queue status.

tags.name: health
tags.description: Health and readiness probes. Kubernetes-compatible /live, /ready, /health endpoints.

tags.name: extensions
tags.description: Extension system status. View middleware pipeline, enable/disable extensions, track execution timeline.

tags.name: domains
tags.description: Domain plugin information. List registered domains and their agents/tools/crews.

tags.name: plugins
tags.description: Plugin system management. List loaded plugins, view plugin details, and check system status.

tags.name: commands
tags.description: Slash command system. List and execute /commands from chat interface.

tags.name: marketplace
tags.description: Plugin marketplace. Browse available plugins, check tier availability, and install.

tags.name: system
tags.description: System information. API version and compatibility data for cross-service checks.
